# Questions in Livestream 24

Live Stream #24: The 24 Thrones

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=330" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:30</a> | Were Jewish ideas of monotheism stolen from Zoroastrianism? | No. He argued Zoroastrianism is dualistic rather than truly monotheistic, and that Israelite monotheism grows out of the Sinai covenant and Ten Commandments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:16</a> | Does anyone know what denomination he is? | He declined to name a denomination and only said he is an evangelical Christian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:47</a> | If extraterrestrial life were confirmed, would that create theological problems? | Only for some systems. He said it would especially strain forms of theology tied to strict young-earth and federated-responsibility models, but not Christian theology as a whole. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=862" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:22</a> | Was secret knowledge normally passed from father to eldest son in Egypt, and would the tenth plague have caused a sudden loss of knowledge? | Passing priestly and ritual knowledge to the heir was normal, but he did not think the tenth plague would have caused an abrupt civilizational knowledge collapse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=1141" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:01</a> | Is there evidence for new Israelite settlement and culture in Canaan after the conquest, and would we expect to see it archaeologically? | Yes, chiefly in faunal remains and later pottery, though he stressed that archaeology sees conquest poorly when incoming groups occupy existing cities rather than destroying them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=1507" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:07</a> | Is Adam's son Seth a polemical representation of the Egyptian god Set? | No. He said the names are not genuinely related and that the biblical Seth does not function like the Egyptian storm god Set. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=1891" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:31</a> | Are the Canaanite god El and Yahweh the same deity, or did Israelites merge them over time? | He treated `el` as both a generic word for god and a proper divine name in Ugaritic religion, while holding that Yahweh is distinct and not simply a later fusion with El. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=2222" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:02</a> | What are your thoughts on the authorship of Isaiah, and is there evidence against multiple authorship? | He said the question depends on the evidence, but he was more inclined toward seeing intentional poetic reflection across Isaiah than toward the standard multiple-authorship argument. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=2496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:36</a> | Did Amenhotep III use Avaris as a summer residence? | He said the question misunderstands Egyptian royal palaces and rejected the idea that Avaris functioned for Amenhotep III in that simple "summer residence" sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=2854" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:34</a> | Why do you reject the documentary hypothesis, including the claim that J and E explain confusing parts of the Joseph narrative? | He rejected it on evidential grounds, saying its criteria are ad hoc and that some alleged contradictions can be better explained from ancient Near Eastern context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=3108" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:48</a> | Could you comment on the labyrinth of Egypt, and are excavations ongoing? | He linked Herodotus's labyrinth to sun-temple remains and said a maze-like structure seems to exist, but he did not know of current excavation there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=3294" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:54</a> | What does Gobekli Tepe suggest about very early agrarian civilization? | He said it is very early and likely tied to emerging agriculture, while noting Anatolian prehistory is outside his main field. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=3495" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:15</a> | Can the Ketef Hinnom amulets be used as evidence for the antiquity of the Torah? | Yes. He said they show written Torah material in the 7th century BCE and therefore push against claims that the Torah remained only oral until exile. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=3728" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:08</a> | Can you explain how Psalm 110 reflects ancient Near Eastern royal and priestly iconography? | He said Psalm 110 is full of royal imagery, including enthronement, enemies as a footstool, scepters, conquest, and Melchizedek as a king-priest figure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=4078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:58</a> | Why did Joseph not have his body buried in Canaan like Jacob, and could a travel ban after Ahmose I explain it? | He said the travel restrictions likely came after Joseph's death; the more likely issue was that Joseph's descendants lacked the military resources to transport a valuable mummified body safely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=4274" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:14</a> | If Set became important to Ramesside pharaohs, why would kings who valued ma'at want to be associated with him? | He argued ma'at was less central before Dynasty 19 than often claimed, and that Ramesside ideology used Set within its own Delta-based theological agenda. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=4700" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:20</a> | Does the proposed meteor at Tall el-Hammam have any real evidential value for Sodom? | No. He was sharply critical of the excavation claims, chronology, and published data handling, and did not regard the airburst identification as credible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=4968" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:48</a> | What are the top reasons Amenhotep III was not the pharaoh of the Exodus? | He pointed to secure chronology, continued Semitic occupation at Avaris, Ramesside place names in Exodus, and later sites like Migdol that do not fit Amenhotep III. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=5165" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:05</a> | How does anyone get readings like "Pharaoh means double-minded" or `Mitzrayim` meaning "limiting God"? | He rejected those claims as bad allegorical exegesis and said `Mitzrayim` is simply the normal Semitic name for Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=5438" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:38</a> | How did he decide what to study in university, and when did he become a Christian? | He became a Christian at age seven and said he discovered archaeology and related humanities through trial, remedial detours, and finally noticing what he most loved to read. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=5703" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:03</a> | Who is "So king of Egypt" in 2 Kings 17:4? | He identified him with Osorkon IV on chronological grounds. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=5885" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:05</a> | What does `elohim` mean in Exodus 21:6, and is there any Egyptian context? | He leaned toward understanding the scene as a vow administered before a Levite rather than as a temple or household-deity ritual. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=6665" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:05</a> | What books do you recommend for starting to learn about the ancient Near East, and how can someone judge whether sources are good or bad? | He said judging sources takes specialized experience, but recommended starting with responsible scholarly works such as ancient Near Eastern background books, Hess on Israelite religion, *Context of Scripture*, Ian Shaw, John Walton, and Alan Millard. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=6866" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:26</a> | Is that a scotch glass? | Yes, technically, though he said it was full of water during the stream. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=6921" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:21</a> | Is Deuteronomy 22:5 referring to cult practice, and how should it be read in context? | He said it belongs with the surrounding case laws and should first be read contextually rather than detached into a specialized cultic theory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/8ADMb8ZtUiQ?t=7412" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:32</a> | Is it true that the Great Pyramid was not built by slaves? | Yes. He said the evidence points instead to a skilled, provisioned craftsman class living in workers' settlements such as the one excavated at Giza. |
